title: A Year of Thirst
article_text: It is a listener who feels a deep thirst on Monday. On Tuesday, the tender heat of March made the density of the air feel heavy. Wednesday in July brought a thirst that could not be ignored. It was on Thursday that the ugly module of the old pump finally broke. Friday in September had a thirst so strong that even the animals were quiet. Saturday in January is when we should have fixed the pump. Sunday in February was cold, but the thirst did not go away. Not only did April and May pass, but also June and August without enough rain. Only then, in October, did the first rain fall, and the thirst was ended. Had November and December been dry, the land would have been lost.

image_prompts: A person listening intently on a Monday morning, looking thirsty.
Tender heat of March with heavy air density, a thermometer showing high temperature.
A hot Wednesday in July with a person showing thirst, wiping sweat.
An ugly broken pump module on a Thursday, with a farmer looking sad.
A dry Friday in September, animals quiet around an empty water trough.
A cold Saturday in January, a broken pump with ice around it.
A cold Sunday in February, a person drinking from a bottle but still thirsty.
A calendar showing April, May, June, and August passing with no rain clouds.
First rain falling in October, a person catching raindrops happily.
A dry cracked land in November and December, with a lost sign.
